arts.ttl: Millwall club record signing Neghli set for debut as boss Neil throws down challenge to his squad
arts.sum: I don’t think Ra’ees, or anybody, would have thought he would be playing first-team football like he has done – but that’s based around how he’s trained and played. “It’s about bringing some younger players in and trying to create assets.

arts.ttl: Behind-the-scenes Cameron Archer reaction revealed after Leeds United’s failed January bid
arts.sum: Leeds failed to sign Cameron Archer on loan after Southampton rejected bid Cameron Archer of Southampton was the man Leeds turned to with a formal approach, submitting a loan offer that the Saints ended up rejecting.

arts.ttl: Leeds United have new Championship promotion race advantage over Sheffield United and Sunderland
arts.sum: The pressure is on Sheffield United and Sunderland in the Championship promotion race this weekend after Leeds United moved five points clear at the top of the table in midweek. However, the pressure will be on in the Steel City, especially if the Black Cats beat Watford in the early kick-off to move level on points with them and the Clarets.

arts.ttl: Leeds faithful may finally learn answer to Brenden Aaronson transfer question
arts.sum: Farke added: “Of course we need a little bit of luck that [Aaronson] stays fit and in shape, with no injuries. Farke may deem the interim period between the fixtures as enough rest time for him, which could tempt him to start Aaronson against Millwall.

arts.ttl: Leeds United squad revealed for Millwall with one missing from FA Cup tie
arts.sum: The likes of Isaac Scmidt and Josuha Guilavogui will be spying their second starts of the season, having made the starting XI for the first time since the 1-0 over Harrogate Town in the third round of the FA Cup. Rising youngsters James Debayo and Sam Chambers may also be hopeful of minutes. Leeds United squad vs Millwall: Meslier, Darlow, Cairns, Struijk, Rodon, Wober, Firpo, Schmidt, Debayo, Bogle, Byram, Ampadu, Gruev, Guilavogui, Tanaka, Rothwell, Chambers, Aaronson, Solomon, Ramazani, Gnonto, James, Piroe, Joseph.

arts.ttl: Millwall hooligans 'climbed out of toilet windows' for street fight with Leeds fans
arts.sum: A former football hardman has shed light on a fiery street fight between Leeds United and Millwall hooligans, citing how bottles were raining down on the streets of London as two firms came together

arts.ttl: Josuha Guilavogui gives shoutout to Leeds United hero v Coventry as stunning footage emerges
arts.sum: That was largely down to Leeds’ inability to make totally sure of the result but converting their many second half chances, but the Sky Blues looked to claw their way back into the game when Ellis Simms turned a header towards the bottom corner, only to be thwarted by an incredible outstretched arm from Illan Meslier: Watch: Illan Meslier produces two brilliant saves to keep Leeds clean sheet v Coventry

arts.ttl: Leeds United announce shock signing as goalkeeper seals transfer
arts.sum: Leeds United have announced the signing of Australia under-20 goalkeeper Robbie Cook from native club Perth Glory on a two-and-a-half year professional deal. A statement from Leeds United read: “Leeds United are pleased to announce the signing of goalkeeper Robbie Cook, who joins the club from Australian outfit Perth Glory.

arts.ttl: Leeds vs Millwall Prediction: Team News, Predicted Lineups and Where To Watch
arts.sum: Championship leaders Leeds will be hoping to seal their place in the next round of the FA Cup when they welcome Millwall to Elland Road on Saturday afternoon. Ahead of the match, we’ve made ...

arts.ttl: Leeds United confirm Robbie Cook signing
arts.sum: Leeds United have confirmed the signing of young goalkeeper Robbie Cook on a permanent deal from Perth Glory. It was reported back in January that Leeds had handed a trial to 18-year-old Australian goalkeeper Robbie Cook, coming over from Perth Glory to Thorp Arch.
